scope: Read-only source-prep backlog review artifact. No raw blocks, canonical records, submissions, manifests, LOG, materialized output, or generated validation output were mutated.

classification_counts.note: The not-ready readiness rows have failure reasons but omit block_count fields; zero/partial status was verified from raw/sources block files and Chandra cache state.

spreadsheet_backlog.status: All spreadsheet backlog sources have staged spreadsheet files and zero source blocks.

cli_prepare_source.summary: The prepare-source command calls prepareSource(sourceDir, { sourceId }) and prints copied files, optional text path, and block count.

prepare_source.summary: prepareSource copies top-level files into raw/sources/<source_id>, writes staged metadata, then writes blocks from Chandra OCR for PDFs or from a readable text surface for non-PDFs.

non_pdf_text_surface.summary: extractTextSurface only accepts existing non-empty text.txt, source.json rendered as JSON text, or source.html rendered through htmlToText.

text_block_generation.summary: buildSourceBlocksFromText splits text by form feed into pages and by line into text/list/heading blocks with source-relative raw offsets.

pdf_block_generation.summary: writeSourceBlocks writes Chandra OCR blocks for any source with source.pdf; rebuildSourceBlocks repeats the same deterministic write from cached Chandra pages.

chandra_queue_run.summary: queueChandraOcr classifies PDFs as queued, partial, complete, or skipped from source.pdf, chandra/manifest.json, and healthy page JSON files. runChandraOcr can call an external command, so it was not run for this review.

source_intake_stageable_surface.summary: hasStageableSurface accepts text.txt, source.pdf, source.html, or source.json. Existing staged spreadsheets are present in raw/sources, but source-prep has no spreadsheet text-surface branch.

spreadsheet_support.dependency_check: No xlsx/sheetjs/read-excel/spreadsheet parser dependency or spreadsheet-specific source-prep code was found in package.json files or TypeScript sources.

spreadsheets.proposal: Add a deterministic, opt-in spreadsheet text extraction path that writes reviewable sidecar prep artifacts before touching raw blocks.
safe_sequence: Introduce a source-prep command or option that reads raw/sources/<source_id>/source.xlsx or source.xls and writes data/source-prep/<run_id>/<source_id>/spreadsheet-preview.json plus spreadsheet-preview.md.
Represent workbooks as sheets, used ranges, rows, columns, cell values, formulas, merged ranges, and a bounded Markdown/TSV preview; record parser version and file hash.
Run the preview generator on 3-5 pilots and review output quality without modifying raw/sources.
After review, add a separate explicit apply step that converts approved previews into text.txt and blocks.jsonl, preserving raw cell addresses in block metadata or raw text.
notes: Because no spreadsheet parser exists today, the first mutation should be a small dependency and sidecar-only artifact, not direct raw/sources block generation.
Prefer xlsx first because 266 of 267 spreadsheet sources are .xlsx; handle the lone .xls as either a separate parser path or explicit unsupported/quarantined status until tested.

pdfs.proposal: Use a dry-run/sidecar repair manifest for the 23 PDFs, splitting zero-cache PDFs from partial-cache PDFs.
safe_sequence: For zero PDFs, write a data/source-prep/<run_id>/pdf-queue-preview.json entry with source id, PDF hash, page_count unknown, and required OCR action; do not call external OCR in this lane.
For partial PDFs, write a data/source-prep/<run_id>/pdf-repair-preview.json entry with missing/unhealthy page numbers, existing block count, and candidate repair action.

When provider/external OCR commands are allowed, rerun only missing pages for partials and full OCR for the two zero PDFs, then rebuild source blocks from Chandra cache in a separate explicit apply step.
Do not relax assertSourceReadyForIngest to accept partial PDFs; the current all-pages-ready gate prevents evidence gaps.
notes: Several partial PDFs are one to four pages short and are good repair candidates.
Very large partial PDFs, especially meeting_doc_73241, should be treated as repair-only for the missing pages rather than full reruns.

pilot_source_ids.rationale: Smallest observed xlsx backlog item, a compact Fuel Hedge Program workbook, useful for validating basic workbook-to-preview extraction.
The only .xls source in the 267-spreadsheet backlog; include it early to decide whether legacy Excel support is in scope or should be quarantined.
One of two PDF sources with no Chandra manifest/pages and zero blocks; validates zero-cache queue/preview handling.
Manageable 72-page PDF with 71 healthy pages and only page 57 missing; good smallest repair-flow pilot.
Large PDF with exactly one missing page, testing whether single-page repair scales without rerunning hundreds of healthy pages.
